title: miliusa gokhalaei
intro: Miliusa gokhalaei, also known as the Himalayan milu, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been found to possess significant medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Miliusa gokhalaei include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as an antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments.
